label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about what the passage says regarding the search for a missing child. The options are A to D. First, I need to recall the passage. It starts by listing various methods they use: putting an APB, calling hospitals, contacting parents, friends, checking last seen in papers, posters, TV, and a magazine. Then it says that with nearly two million missing children every year, they can't do all these things for everyone because of time, money, and personnel constraints. The question asks what they do according to the passage. The options are: A) can always find him – the passage doesn't say they can always find the child. In fact, it implies that sometimes they can't do all the steps. B) couldn't always try every way mentioned above – this seems to align with the part where it says they can't do all these things for everyone because of limitations. So maybe B is correct. C) just try one way or another – the passage lists multiple methods they use, not just one, so C might not be right. D) won't give up until they find him – the passage doesn't mention persistence
